long_stay: Unpaid vocational training/internship programs (as a trainee)
qualifying_courses.notes: For higher education, exchange, and pre-university programs, the institution must be accredited or provisionally authorized by Romanian law, and the program must be full-time. For trainees, the official requirement is a training agreement with a host entity (plus a written commitment from that entity), not institutional accreditation.

booking_methods.description: Official electronic portal of the Romanian Ministry of Foreign Affairs

steps.title: Apply to a Romanian university and receive an offer/admission letter
Receive the official Letter of Acceptance issued by the Romanian Ministry of Education
Pay at least one year’s tuition fee (unless exempted)
Register and apply online via the E-VISA portal
Upload supporting documents and wait for the file to be validated
Book and attend an appointment at the relevant Romanian embassy/consulate
steps.description: Submit original documents, provide biometric data, and pay the visa fee.

steps.title: Wait for the decision on the visa application

required_documents.name: Valid Passport
required_documents.details: Valid for at least 3 months after the intended date of departure from Romania; original and copy

required_documents.name: Official Letter of Acceptance for Studies
required_documents.details: Issued by the Romanian Ministry of Education (not just university offer letter)

required_documents.name: Proof of Tuition Fee Payment
required_documents.details: For at least one academic year (unless exempted)

required_documents.name: Criminal Record Certificate
required_documents.details: Issued by authorities in the country of origin, or equivalent legal document. Must be translated by a certified translator and legalized or apostilled.

required_documents.name: Medical Insurance
required_documents.details: Covering the entire period of visa validity, with a minimum coverage of 30,000 EUR

required_documents.name: Parental or Guardian Consent
required_documents.details: For applicants under 18 years of age

required_documents.name: Proof of Accommodation
required_documents.details: e.g., dormitory contract, lease agreement, or notarized statement from a host

required_documents.name: Proof of Language Knowledge
required_documents.details: Not required for those taking the Romanian-language preparatory year

financial_requirements.reference_basis: Minimum gross basic salary per month (Government Decision (HG) no. 146/2026)
acceptable_proof: Bank statements from a personal account
exemptions: Scholarship holders at accredited public universities

residence_permit.booking_method: In person at local IGI office or via online portal (portaligi.mai.gov.ro)
required_documents: Letter of acceptance for studies (only needed for the first extension)
Certificate from the educational institution confirming full-time enrollment
Proof of means of support (at least 6 months’ worth)
Proof of legal right to accommodation/living space (original and copy)
residence_permit.notes: The IGI must resolve the application within 30 days of submission (extendable by up to 15 days if further checks are needed).

restrictions: Work must be part-time under an individual employment contract.
Exceeding the permitted daily hours requires a separate full work permit.
work_while_studying.notes: The official limit for students working without a separate work authorization is up to 6 hours per day, according to current IGI guidance. Some third-party sources may cite an older limit of 4 hours per day (20 hours/week). It is recommended to confirm the current limit directly with the IGI or your local Immigration Bureau before starting work.

required_documents: Certificate from the university confirming enrollment in the next year of study
Other documents as per initial application (except Ministry of Education acceptance letter)
permit_renewal.notes: The right of residence can be extended for a period equal to the total duration of the study program through successive renewals. For exchange program participants, extensions are capped at one year at a time. If a student does not finish their studies within the originally granted period, a further extension to complete studies can be granted, not exceeding one additional year.

requirements: Find an employer willing to hire them
Employer must obtain a work authorization from the IGI
Apply for a change in residence permit purpose from 'studies' to 'work'
post_study_work.notes: The student-purpose residence permit itself is not directly convertible into a long-term residence permit; the change of purpose (study → work) is treated as a new residence permit application based on employment, even though it can build on the post-graduation extension period. Separately, the permit may be extended for up to 6 months after graduation purely for administrative reasons (e.g., to finish authentication of diplomas/transcripts).

official_sources.name: IGI Studies page
official_sources.description: Primary source for study-related immigration information

application_forms.name: Residence Permit Forms
application_forms.description: Includes application for extension of temporary right of residence, certificate from educational institution, proof of means of subsistence, proof of legal right to dwelling space, medical certificate, and fee schedule document.

tips.title: Ensure Official Acceptance Letter
tips.body: A university admission/offer letter is not sufficient; the official Letter of Acceptance from the Romanian Ministry of Education is mandatory for the visa process.

tips.title: Apply Early
tips.body: Submit your visa application well in advance, ideally at least two months before your intended travel date, to account for the 30-60 day processing time.

tips.title: Mind Passport Validity
tips.body: Ensure your passport is valid for at least three months beyond your intended date of departure from Romania.

tips.title: Prepare Financial Proof Carefully
tips.body: Provide clear bank statements showing your name and the required minimum gross salary amount for the entire period of your visa/permit. Incomplete or outdated proof can cause delays.

tips.title: Translate and Legalize Documents
tips.body: All official documents (e.g., criminal records, diplomas) must be translated by a certified translator and legalized or apostilled.

tips.title: Do Not Miss Residence Permit Deadlines
tips.body: Apply for your residence permit within 90 days of arrival and at least 30 days before your entry visa expires. For renewals, apply at least 30 days before the current permit expires. Missing these deadlines can lead to fines or legal complications.

tips.title: Budget for Tuition Payment Timing
tips.body: Visa approval typically requires proof that at least one year’s tuition has already been paid, not just an offer of admission.

tips.title: Maintain Accommodation Paperwork
tips.body: Proof of legal right to housing is required at each residence permit renewal; ensure you have a proper rental contract or notarized statement for your accommodation.

tips.title: Understand Work Limits
tips.body: Adhere strictly to the permitted part-time work hours (currently up to 6 hours per day) on a study residence permit. Exceeding this limit without a separate work authorization can jeopardize your legal status.

tips.title: Plan Post-Graduation Status Change
tips.body: If you wish to stay and work after graduation, actively pursue the employer-sponsored work authorization process within the post-graduation extension window, as the study residence permit does not automatically convert to a work permit.

common_mistakes.mistake: Skipping the Ministry of Education acceptance letter
common_mistakes.consequence: A university admission/offer letter is not sufficient for the visa process, leading to rejection.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Ensure this separate official document is issued before applying for the visa.

common_mistakes.mistake: Applying for the visa too late
common_mistakes.consequence: Risk missing the start of the academic term due to the 30-60 day visa processing time.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Submit your application at least two months before your intended travel date.

common_mistakes.mistake: Missing the residence permit application deadline after arrival
common_mistakes.consequence: Can lead to fines, legal complications, or the obligation to leave the country.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Apply for the residence permit within 90 days of arrival and at least 30 days before your entry visa expires.

common_mistakes.mistake: Providing incomplete or inconsistent financial proof
common_mistakes.consequence: Common cause of delay or rejection, as the amount must meet the current minimum gross salary threshold for the entire period.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Ensure bank statements clearly show your name and the required monthly minimum amount, and are up-to-date.

common_mistakes.mistake: Not budgeting for tuition payment timing
common_mistakes.consequence: Visa approval typically requires proof that at least one year’s tuition has already been paid, not just an offer of admission.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Arrange for tuition payment before submitting your visa application.

common_mistakes.consequence: Proof of legal right to housing is required at each residence permit renewal, and informal arrangements can cause problems.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Maintain a proper rental contract or notarized statement for your accommodation.

common_mistakes.mistake: Exceeding the part-time work limit on a study permit
common_mistakes.consequence: Can jeopardize the resident’s legal status and lead to penalties.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Adhere strictly to the permitted part-time hours (currently up to 6 hours per day) or obtain a separate full work permit if working more.

common_mistakes.mistake: Forgetting the 30-day renewal window for the residence permit
common_mistakes.consequence: Renewing late, or after the permit has already expired, creates legal and administrative complications.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Submit your renewal application to the IGI at least 30 days before your current permit expires.

common_mistakes.mistake: Assuming the study residence permit automatically converts into a work permit
common_mistakes.consequence: Students wishing to stay and work after graduation need to actively pursue a new employer-sponsored work authorization process.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Understand that a change of purpose is treated as a new residence permit application based on employment, and plan accordingly within the post-graduation extension window.
